decisions.default: Implement P0, review P1, waive P2 unless consumer appears.
decisions.item: Which pointer-level-only datasets need full reader support?

decisions.default: No. Default retain + superseded until dependency proof and archive/rollback plan exist.
decisions.item: Can any legacy canonical run be deleted?

decisions.default: Yes, dry-run only; no sync/delete without explicit gate.
decisions.item: Should recurring validation include NAS dry-run?

decisions.default: Prefer explicit config correction after secret-safe review. Review all 4 root variables and .env.example key consistency; wrappers continue fail-closed.
decisions.item: Should .env be edited or should wrappers override stale config?

decisions.default: Run read-only extension assessment first, then implement P0 support only after registration/schema/performance scope is known.
decisions.item: Should W3-A implement reader P0 immediately or first run reader API extension assessment?

release_slices.value: Research users can consume liquidity_capacity and market_cap; operations no longer trip over stale lake root config.
Future publish/catalog changes fail closed and historical runs remain traceable.
Current truth remains continuously verifiable; pointer-only waivers are explicit; conditional non-PIT reader support is visible rather than counted as default available.
Provider catalog remains deferred unless a real production consumer appears; if one appears, Gate G becomes a separate explicit write gate.

industry_classification.reason: Current CR139 lake industry_classification rows are non_pit_snapshot; default reader must fail closed to avoid PIT misuse.

workstreams.exit_criteria: Reader API extension assessment PASS; P0 datasets have explicit implementation scope and schema/performance smoke plan; reader smoke covers every dataset that has a real consumer; waived datasets have documented pointer-level-only rationale; conditional readers record default status, allow-warn status, issue codes, and row-count evidence.
workstreams.name: Reader support expansion

scope: locate read_dataset supported dataset registration path
verify schema compatibility contract for liquidity_capacity and market_cap
define row-count and reader smoke strategy
assess performance risk for 17M-row P0 datasets

workstreams.exit_criteria: A machine-readable superseded register exists; delete remains blocked unless dependency proof is 0 and rollback/archive plan exists.
workstreams.name: Legacy retention and superseded register

workstreams.exit_criteria: A single guard command can fail-closed before pointer advance or active catalog refresh.
workstreams.name: Publish guard automation

workstreams.exit_criteria: Validation pack is repeatable and produces stable PASS/WARN/BLOCKED outputs.

workstreams.exit_criteria: All 4 roots point to data-lake-aligned paths; .env and .env.example are key-consistent for lake/archive/backup/restore; guarded wrappers fail closed on stale config and never print secrets.
